title: Enhanced analytics: frequency polygons, subject stats, nine-scale grading, and corrected combined percentage

requirements.text: In the View Progress tab, add a frequency polygon chart that plots the distribution of original percentage scores. The chart must be rendered per term and per grade (i.e., one frequency polygon per grade-term combination visible after filtering). Use class intervals (e.g., 0-10, 10-20, …, 90-100) on the X-axis and frequency (number of students/entries) on the Y-axis, with connected line points.

quotes: frequency polygon for original percentage and for each term of each grade

acceptanceCriteria: A frequency polygon (line chart with data points) is displayed in the View Progress tab for each grade-term combination.
X-axis represents percentage intervals, Y-axis represents frequency counts.
The chart updates when the user filters by grade or term.
The chart uses the original (unadjusted) percentage values.

requirements.text: In the View Progress tab, display the average percentage for each grade as a summary statistic. This should appear as a clearly labeled figure (e.g., 'Grade 5 Average: 78.4%') either in the existing table/chart area or as summary cards above or below the chart.
acceptanceCriteria: Average percentage is computed and displayed for each grade present in the filtered data.
The label clearly identifies the grade.
Average updates correctly when term or grade filters are changed.

requirements.text: In the Subject Analysis tab, display the average raw marks (e.g., '73') for each subject across all grades alongside the existing average percentage. The raw mark average must be computed from actual stored marks divided by the number of entries for that subject, and shown as a numeric value (e.g., 'Avg Marks: 73').

quotes: average raw marks like 73 of each subject of all grades alongside average percentage

acceptanceCriteria: Each subject row/bar in Subject Analysis shows both average raw marks and average percentage.
Average raw marks are displayed as a rounded numeric value.
The value is computed from actual stored raw marks, not derived from percentage.

requirements.text: In the Subject Analysis tab, display the highest and lowest raw marks and the highest and lowest percentage for each subject, with the ability to filter by grade and term. The stats (highest mark, lowest mark, highest %, lowest %) must be shown per subject for the selected grade and term combination.

quotes: highest and lowest of each subject alongside lowest and highest percentage of each grade and should be filter through grade and term

acceptanceCriteria: Subject Analysis tab has grade and term filter controls.
For each subject, the UI displays: highest raw marks, lowest raw marks, highest percentage, lowest percentage.
All four stats update correctly when grade or term filter changes.
Stats are computed only from entries matching the selected grade and term.

requirements.text: In the Nine Grading System (9-Scale Grades) tab, display the computed 9-scale grade (1–9) for each subject for each term of each grade. The grading scale must be: 90–100 = 9, 80–89 = 8, 70–79 = 7, 60–69 = 6, 50–59 = 5, 40–49 = 4, 30–39 = 3, 20–29 = 2, 0–19 = 1. Each subject cell must show the grade number (e.g., '8') computed from the subject's percentage for that entry's term.

quotes: nine grading system there should be grading for each subject of each term of each grade like 90-100 is 9 80-89 is 8 so on

acceptanceCriteria: Each subject for each entry (per term, per grade) shows its 9-scale grade.
Grades are visible per term and per grade, with filter controls to narrow by grade and term.
The gradeCalculation utility is updated or extended to use the new 9-point boundaries.

requirements.text: In the Combined Percentage tab, replace the current fixed formula with a dynamic calculation that averages the calculated (adjusted) percentages of all available terms for a student/entry group. If only Term 1 and Term 2 exist, divide the sum by 2. If Term 3 also exists, divide the sum by 3. The result is the overall combined percentage. Update the display and the calculation logic in both the utility functions (percent.ts or equivalent) and the CombinedPercentageView component.

quotes: combined percentage after calculated percentage is found it is should be calculated on basis of overall percentage by combining all calculated percentage of every term and divide by 2 or 3 if term 3 is mentioned

acceptanceCriteria: Combined percentage is computed by summing calculated percentages of all terms present and dividing by the count of distinct terms (2 or 3).
If Term 3 data exists for a student, the divisor is 3; otherwise it is 2.
The CombinedPercentageView table shows the correctly recalculated combined percentage.
The percent.ts utility is updated to support this dynamic term-count division.
Old hardcoded formula (original + 94) / 2 is replaced or clearly separated from the new combined percentage logic.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All chart rendering must use existing charting libraries already in the project (e.g., Recharts) — do not add new charting dependencies unless unavoidable.
Backend data model changes should be minimal; prefer computing new statistics on the frontend from existing stored data.
nonGoals: Adding new student/entry data fields to the backend schema.
Changing the certificate tier logic in CertificateView.
Modifying the board exam (Grade 10/12) calculation logic.
Changing authentication or user management flows.
